meta.title: Software Engineer | Distributed Systems & SaaS | SAIFCORE
meta.description: Software engineer & technical consultant in Dakar — I help teams ship and evolve Java / cloud-native systems for SaaS and enterprise: clearer architecture, safer delivery, less rework after launch. Senegal & international engagements.
meta.ogTitle: Software Engineer — Distributed Systems & Scalable SaaS
meta.ogDescription: Consulting and hands-on delivery for SaaS and enterprise platforms — Java, Spring, cloud-native. Based in Senegal; open to national and international collaboration. A fit matters more than a pitch.
meta.twitterDescription: Java & cloud-native · Dakar — consulting & delivery for systems that outlast demo day.
meta.jsonLdDescription: Software engineer and consultant in Dakar, Senegal. Designs and delivers business-critical systems with Java and cloud-native architectures — SaaS, enterprise, and distributed services. Available for advisory, embedded delivery, and phased engagements in Senegal and internationally (remote, hybrid, or on-site when appropriate).
meta.manifestName: SAIFCORE — Software Engineer | Distributed Systems & SaaS
meta.manifestDescription: Java & cloud-native systems for SaaS & enterprise — consulting and delivery from architecture through production. Senegal & international.

hero.subtitle: Launch week is only the beginning. I partner with product and engineering teams on Java and cloud-native systems — from architecture through production — so roadmap pressure doesn’t undo what already works.
hero.locationLine: Dakar, Senegal — on-site or hybrid locally; remote or hybrid internationally. Overlap-friendly across Francophone Africa, Europe, and US time zones when it matters.
hero.nextSteps: Intro call → written scope, risks & constraints → proposal or kickoff. Clear steps, no endless email tennis.
hero.jumpToContactHint: email, CV, and full booking options.

trust.heading: Teams that needed shipping in production — not another roadmap deck

whatISolve.title: What I solve
whatISolve.subtitle: What I bring to consulting and long-term collaboration: clear ownership, delivery you can plan around, and fewer surprises when load or regulation tighten the screws.

items.title: Architecture & system design
items.description: Boundaries your team can explain in a stand-up — so “one more feature” doesn’t quietly become a six-month rewrite.

items.title: Scalable SaaS & enterprise platforms
items.description: Multi-tenant reality: integrations, billing, and ops you’ll still recognize after the next funding round.

items.title: Distributed & business-critical systems
items.description: Money, latency, audits — the flows where “we’ll patch it later” isn’t an option. Built deliberately, not improvised.

items.title: Engineering excellence
items.description: Tests, CI/CD, and signals you can trust — so Friday deploys are boring for the right reasons.

howIThink.title: How I think
howIThink.subtitle: From vague brief to something running in production — with only the process your risk level actually needs.

steps.title: Frame the problem
steps.body: Who decides, what’s non-negotiable, what “good” means — before we pick a framework logo.

steps.title: Shape the architecture
steps.body: Boundaries, interfaces, and a plausible next year so your team isn’t hostage to my first diagram.

steps.title: Ship with guardrails
steps.body: Tests, CI, and alerts aligned with how you’ll really run it — not how the demo looked.

steps.title: Harden against reality
steps.body: Measure and fix where users and revenue hurt — not where the Grafana chart is prettiest.

howIThink.operatingBody: Architecture is a product: written decisions when they reduce risk, measurable outcomes, and room for your team to own the system — consulting should leave you stronger, not dependent.
bullets: ADRs and diagrams when they prevent expensive misunderstandings
SLOs, tracing, and alerts tied to real user journeys
Security and compliance as design inputs — not a late panic

featuredProjects.title: Featured projects
featuredProjects.subtitle: References you can verify — public names when NDAs allow. Each card mirrors a type of engagement: similar constraints, delivery pressure, and outcomes you can discuss before we scope yours.
featuredProjects.bridgeTitle: Planning a build, refactor, or scale-up?
featuredProjects.bridgeSubtitle: Share the goal and hard constraints — I’ll outline options, risks, and a realistic first step so you can decide on fit before any formal commitment.

items.title: Ecom 360 PME
items.context: What if stock, sales, and the messy day-to-day lived in one product instead of five tabs and a spreadsheet? SaaS for retail SMEs — built to retire the accidental tool stack.
items.impact: Merchants productive in days, not quarters — onboarding that sidesteps the “big bang” IT programme while still caring about traceability.

items.title: FitnessOS
items.context: Who owns the gym’s truth — the front desk laptop or the member’s phone? Staff on the web, members on mobile; the product is still baking, but the story is already public.
items.impact: One spine for operations, members, and multi-site expansion — fewer handoffs, fewer “we’ll fix it in the next tool”.

items.title: Multi-tenant B2B SaaS
items.context: Greenfield B2B: tenants, roles, usage-based billing — and a domain you can split before the merge queue becomes your roadmap. The interesting question is how modular the first cut is.
items.impact: ~40% faster feature cadence once boundaries landed — releases that didn’t need a committee merge every time.

experience.title: Experience
experience.subtitle: Banking, fintech, high-traffic products — contexts where releases, compliance, and incidents all show up in the same week. Java/Spring, Angular, React.
experience.valueTitle: What you get in a collaboration
experience.valueBody: Hands-on experience on regulated stacks, mobile-money integrations, and real delivery pipelines — so our work together starts from practice, not theory. Useful when you need judgment under pressure, not another generic blueprint.

items.company: BANK OF AFRICA — BOA Services International
items.bullet0: Critical-path Java/Spring banking modules for analytics and operational reporting — scalability, performance, and resilience as first-class constraints.
items.bullet1: Jasper Reports for decision-grade reporting; latency and availability work paired with business stakeholders on complex requirements.
items.bullet2: Java, Spring Boot, Jasper Reports, REST APIs, Agile (Scrum)

items.bullet0: Credit and digital-transaction platform with strict security and performance targets; owned the end-to-end credit application flow.
items.bullet1: Wave & Orange Money integrations (~60% faster processing); GitLab CI/CD (~50% faster deploys); unit, integration, and regression coverage; close product partnership.
items.bullet2: Java, Spring Boot, Spring Security, PostgreSQL, GitLab CI/CD, Docker, REST APIs, Swagger, Scrum

items.bullet0: High-traffic fintech for SMEs and informal retailers; Angular UIs for stock and order management.
items.bullet1: Frontend performance (lazy loading, code splitting, caching; ~60% faster); Spring services (~30% faster API responses); UX refresh and technical docs.
items.bullet2: Angular, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, Java, Spring Boot, REST APIs, MySQL, Git, Agile

items.bullet0: Integrated hospital information system (clinic, pharmacy, lab) — Angular, Spring Boot, REST.
items.bullet1: Caching and asynchronous processing; code review and team engineering standards.
items.bullet2: Angular, Spring Boot, REST APIs, asynchronous processing, testing

architectureExpertise.title: Core expertise
architectureExpertise.subtitle: Tools and practices I use in advisory and delivery — chosen for outcomes your team can measure, not for buzzwords.

about.title: About
about.p1: Product intent only matters if the software can still move a year later — explicit trade-offs, boundaries your team can name, and architectures that don’t calcify on day one.
about.p2b: architecture decisions, system design, junior engineers mentoring and engineering practices
about.p2c: that keep delivery calm when complexity and traffic grow.
about.p3: Strong Java & Spring background, modern cloud-native stacks, and JavaScript frameworks when the product needs them — for SaaS, enterprise platforms, mobile applications and web applications.
about.p4: Focus: FinTech, SaaS, e-commerce, digital platforms, and large-scale enterprise. Based in Dakar — engagements in Senegal and internationally. Formats: architecture advisory, embedded delivery with your team, or phased work — scoped to your risk, budget, and timeline. Async-first with clear written specs; live overlap when decisions need it.

insights.title: Insights & thinking
insights.subtitle: Notes on trade-offs, failure modes, and delivery — background reading if you’re evaluating a collaboration or a technical direction.
insights.endCtaTitle: Facing a similar challenge in your stack?
insights.endCtaSubtitle: Use them as a lens — then we can test them against your real constraints in a conversation.

articlesPage.metaDescription: Architecture, SaaS, delivery — practical notes for teams building or scaling products; useful context before a consulting conversation.
articlesPage.title: Articles
articlesPage.subtitle: Essays, code, ADRs, design notes — ideas to adapt to your context, not copy-paste prescriptions.
articlesPage.endCtaTitle: Want to apply this to your product?
articlesPage.endCtaSubtitle: Share your stack, constraints, and timeline — I’ll say clearly if there’s a fit.

cta.title: Tell me what you’re building — and what’s blocking you.
cta.subtitle: Senegal or international: product, constraints, timeline. You’ll get a direct answer — whether there’s a fit, a concrete next step for an engagement, or an honest no so neither side wastes time.
cta.processStep1: You book a slot or send a brief — context, goals, and timeline.
cta.processStep2: I come back with clarifying questions and, when it fits, a lean scope or options.
cta.processStep3: We align on the next step: discovery workshop, architecture sprint, or phased delivery.
cta.responseHint: I usually acknowledge messages within two business days (often faster).

notFound.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or was moved. Head back to the portfolio to explore work and contact options.
